key_insight: Projection onto SAE feature subspaces is an information bottleneck that removes most task-vector energy, so SAEs are better used to choose where to edit than to perform the edit itself.
engineering_relevance: For practical LLM math editing, the paper offers a deterministic, zero-extra-inference-cost layer-selection strategy that improves targeted performance without full fine-tuning.
method: Tested SAE-guided projection of raw task vectors onto feature subspaces for editing.
Found a geometric mismatch between activation-space SAE directions and weight-space task vectors.
Switched to an SAE-derived layer specificity score to inject unfiltered task vectors only into selected layers.
evidence_signal: On Gemma-3-4B-IT, the projection approach discarded about 97% of modification energy and showed no significant gains across seven math subjects, while layer selection improved Number Theory accuracy from 29.6% to 39.4% on Minerva Math.
watch_item: The method depends on reliable SAE-derived layer specificity; if that diagnostic signal is misestimated or model geometry shifts, the selected layers may not generalize well.
paper_title: Interpretability-Guided Layer Selection over Subspace Projection: SAEs as Stethoscopes, Not Scalpels, for Raw Task Vector Model Editing
